title: Compliance — Research Security: Training Requirement (Policy Angle)
content:
This page documents the Research Security Training requirement from a COMPLIANCE / POLICY angle. The same source URL is covered from a TRAININGS-CATALOG angle in `trainings_compliance_security` (doc_id) inside the trainings collection — see that record for the catalog of approved providers and certificate-retention guidance. Both records intentionally cross-reference: this compliance record explains the regulatory obligation; the trainings record functions as the training catalog entry.

Policy Requirement (verbatim from page): All Morgan State University personnel engaged in federally funded research are required to complete Research Security Training. Individuals are required to complete one of the approved training options listed below. A copy of the completed training certificate must be retained for personal records and may be requested for compliance verification or audit purposes.

Regulatory anchor: The requirement implements National Security Presidential Memorandum 33 (NSPM-33). NSPM-33 requires federal research agencies to ensure that institutions receiving over $50 million annually in federal R&D funding certify they have a research security program — which must include training of relevant personnel on research security threats and insider threats, where applicable. In response, NSF, NIH, DOE, and DOD have developed online research security training, available through the CITI Programs platform among others.

Approved training options (complete ONE):
1. CITI Program — https://citiprogram.org/ (an MSU account will need to be established for access)
2. NSF SECURE Center — https://www.secure-center.org/ctm
3. NSF Research Security Training Modules — https://www.nsf.gov/research-security/training

Compliance enforcement: Certificate retention is the responsibility of the trainee. ORA may request the certificate during audits or to satisfy sponsor compliance documentation.

Adjacent compliance trainings: Other research-compliance trainings (IRB human subjects, IACUC animal use, Responsible Conduct of Research (RCR), Conflict of Interest, Export Controls) are addressed through related ORA compliance pages and are also offered through the CITI Program. Morgan-delivered seminars on RCR, COI, and Export Controls are archived in the Monthly D-RED Seminars catalog.

Cadence: Not specified on this page. Training is required upon engagement with federally funded research and renewed per sponsor or institutional policy.

Contact: Office of Research Administration, Tyler Hall, Student Service Bldg, Suite 304, Baltimore, MD 21251. Phone 443-885-4044. Fax 443-885-8280. Email ask.ora@morgan.edu.

key_facts.required_for: All MSU personnel engaged in federally funded research
